narrative:
Zion Suzuki is the goalkeeper for Ghana, holding an overall rating of 7.31/10. This places him in the top 6% of goalkeepers at this tournament. He has played 270 minutes at this World Cup, establishing himself as a key presence in goal.

His performance at this World Cup includes 2.667 saves per 90 minutes and 1.667 clearances per 90 minutes, demonstrating his active role in defence. His expected assists per 90 minutes is 0.002 at this World Cup and 0.0 at club level, indicating minimal offensive contribution. A substantial 62% of his overall rating comes from his consistent club form over the last two seasons.

Suzuki is clearly Ghana's first-choice goalkeeper, crucial for their defensive stability. Scouts and fans should observe his continued shot-stopping and decision-making, especially given his rating range of 6.9–7.72, as the tournament progresses. His ability to maintain high performance will be vital for Ghana's prospects.
